zh.lifeSummary: 當Hannah Green於1659年12月24日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可馬爾登時，她的父親Thomas Green Jr.29歲，母親Rebecca Hills25歲。她於1677年8月26日與John Vinton在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可馬爾登結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1740年9月30日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼布萊崔享年80歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可雷亞丁衛克菲德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hannah Green于1659年12月24日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可馬爾登时，她的父亲Thomas Green Jr.29岁，母亲Rebecca Hills25岁。她于1677年8月26日与John Vinton在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可馬爾登结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1740年9月30日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼布萊崔享年80岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可雷亞丁衛克菲德。

nameMeanings.name: Green
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: either a nickname for someone who was fond of dressing in this color (Old English grēne) or was young or immature, or who had played the part of the ‘Green Man’ in the May Day celebrations, or a topographic name for someone who lived near a village green (Middle English grene, a transferred use of the color term). This is one of the most common and widespread of English surnames. In North America it has assimilated cognates from other languages, notably German Grün (see Gruen ) and Dutch Groen ; compare 7 below. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English: alternatively, from a Middle English personal name Grene.
Irish: adopted for Ó hUainín ‘descendant of Uainín’, a personal name from a pet form of uaine ‘green’, see Honan .

variants.name: Greenman
meanings: English (Wiltshire): from Middle English grene ‘green’ + man ‘man’, a nickname from the Green Man of folklore, or a topographic name for someone who lived by the village green. Compare Green .

Americanized form (translation into English) of German Grunemann or Grünemann: topographic name for someone who lived by the village green (compare above and Greenland ), or a habitational name for someone from any of numerous places called Grüna, Grünau, or Grüne.

variants.name: Greening
meanings: English (Gloucestershire and Dorset): nickname perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English grening, a derivative of grene ‘green’ (Old English grēne), i.e. ‘the green one’, whether physically or metaphorically ‘green’.
English: alternatively, from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Grening, a derivative (originally patronymic) of the Middle English personal name Grene. See Green .
Americanized form of German Grüning, a variant of Groening .

variants.name: Gruen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Dieter, Dietrich, Georg, Hans, Heinz, Hermann, Hertha.  Jewish Ehud, Eliezer, Isadore, Isidor, Meyer, Rivka, Shimon, Shlomo, Shoshana.

German (Grün): from Middle High German grūene ‘green, fresh, raw’, hence a nickname for someone who habitually dressed in green, a topographic name for someone who lived in a green and leafy place, or a habitational name for someone from a place called with this word, such as Gruna, Grunau in Silesia.
Jewish (Ashkenazic; Grün): artificial name from German grün ‘green’. Compare Grun .

variants.name: Greene
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Green  . In North America, this surname has assimilated cognates from other languages.

nameMeanings.name: Hannah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the mother of the prophet Samuel (1 Samuel 1:2), Hebrew Hanna. It is derived from a Hebrew word meaning ‘He (i.e. God) has favoured me (i.e. with a child)’. See also Anne . This form of the name was taken up as a given name by the Puritans in the 16th and 17th centuries and remained popular until the late 19th century. Thereafter it fell somewhat from favour but has enjoyed a massive revival since the 1990s.

variants.name: Hanna
meanings: Either the biblical name ( see Hannah ) or a short form of Johanna .

variants.name: Hana
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Hannah Green was born on 24 December 1659, in Malden, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, her father, Thomas Green Jr., was 29 and her mother, Rebecca Hills, was 25. She married John Vinton on 26 August 1677, in Malden, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 30 September 1740, in Braintree, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 80, and was buried in Wakefield, Reading, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hannah Green est née le 24 décembre 1659, à Malden, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Thomas Green Jr., avait 29 ans et sa mère, Rebecca Hills, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé John Vinton le 26 août 1677, à Malden, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 30 septembre 1740, à Braintree, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 80 ans, et a été enterrée à Wakefield, Reading, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hannah Green am 24. Dezember 1659 in Malden, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Green Jr. 29 und ihre Mutter Rebecca Hills 25. Sie hat am 26. August 1677 in Malden, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America John Vinton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb am 30. September 1740 im Alter von 80 Jahren in Braintree, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Wakefield, Reading, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Hannah Greenが1659年12月24日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックスマルデンで生まれた時，父Thomas Green Jr.は29歳，母Rebecca Hillsは25歳であった。1677年8月26日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックスマルデンでJohn Vintonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1740年9月30日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーブレイントレで80歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックスレアディングワケフィールドにて埋葬された。

ko.Malden, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Col: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 맬든

ko.Wakefield, Reading, Middlesex, Massachus: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 리딩 웨이크필드
ko.lifeSummary: Hannah Green(이)가 1659년 12월 24일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 맬든에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Green Jr.(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Rebecca Hills(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1677년 8월 26일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 맬든에서 John Vinton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1740년 9월 30일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 브레이트리에서 80세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 리딩 웨이크필드에 묻혔다.

ru.Wakefield, Reading, Middlesex, Massachus: веикефиелд, ридинг, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hannah Green родилась 24 декабря 1659 г. в местности малден, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени Thomas Green Jr. было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Rebecca Hills, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Vinton 26 августа 1677 г. в местности малден, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 30 сентября 1740 г. в местности бреинтри, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 80 лет и была похоронена в местности веикефиелд, ридинг, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hannah Green nació el 24 de diciembre de 1659, en Malden, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, su papá, Thomas Green Jr., tenía 29 años y su mamá, Rebecca Hills, tenía 25 años. Se casó con John Vinton el 26 de agosto de 1677, en Malden, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 30 de septiembre de 1740, en Braintree, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 80 años, y fue sepultada en Wakefield, Reading, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.
